target.title: Plugins: enforce private import boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins could couple directly to private OpenClaw internals or escape their package root through imports, even though the intended contract is the plugin SDK seam.
- Why it matters: That makes plugin behavior depend on unstable host internals and weakens the architecture boundary between plugin code and core.
- What changed: Added an enforced loader-time import policy that allows `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`, declared package dependencies, Node builtins, and in-package relative imports, while blocking private `openclaw/*` imports, undeclared bare packages, and imports that escape the plugin root.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not add process isolation or a sandbox; plugins still run arbitrary JS in-process.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Third-party and bundled plugins now fail to load when they import private OpenClaw package paths, undeclared bare packages, or files outside their own package root.
- Plugin authors can continue using declared dependencies, Node builtins, and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` seams.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Plugin code can no longer import private OpenClaw internals or escape its package root through the loader import path. This is contract hardening, not malicious-plugin sandboxing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader
- Relevant config (redacted): local temp plugin fixtures

### Steps

1. Create a plugin that imports `openclaw/runtime` or `../outside.cjs`.
2. Load it through the plugin loader.
3. Create a plugin that imports `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, a declared dependency, and an in-package helper.

### Expected

- Private OpenClaw imports and root-escaping imports are rejected before plugin execution.
- Declared dependencies, SDK seams, and in-package helpers continue to load.

### Actual

- Matches expected in direct local probes.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm build`
  - repo `pnpm check` pipeline via `scripts/committer`
  - direct `node --import tsx` probe rejecting `openclaw/runtime`
  - direct `node --import tsx` probe allowing declared dependency + local helper import
- Edge cases checked:
  - plugin root escape via relative import
  - declared dependency allowlist from plugin-local `package.json`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full `src/plugins/loader.test.ts` suite completion in this worktree
  - real third-party plugins yet

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Mostly yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) Maybe
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Plugin authors importing private OpenClaw internals must move to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` or declare/package their own dependencies locally.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Revert commit `6a8e3526fa`
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/plugins/import-policy.ts`
  - `src/plugins/loader.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - plugins failing at load with `plugin import policy violation`
  - plugins relying on undeclared dependencies or private `openclaw/*` imports

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - Existing plugins may rely on undeclared bare packages or private host imports and now fail fast.
  - Mitigation:
  - The failure is early and explicit, and the allowed path is narrow: declare the dependency or move to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Current minSdk version was too high, preventing users on older Android devices (API 26-30) from installing the app. Additionally, the UI relied on dynamic color features (Material You) available only in newer Android versions, which would cause crashes or rendering issues on older APIs.
- Why it matters: Lowering the minSdk to 26 (Android 8.0) significantly expands our potential user base while maintaining support for essential modern background limits and security features.
- What changed:

target.title: fix(security): use constant-time comparison for TLS fingerprint verification
target.body:
## Summary

TLS certificate fingerprint verification in `src/gateway/client.ts` uses `!==` (standard JavaScript string equality), which is vulnerable to **timing side-channel attacks** (CWE-208).

An attacker on the network path can measure response time differences to iteratively guess the correct fingerprint byte-by-byte, potentially bypassing TLS pinning for MITM attacks on gateway connections.

## Root cause

Two locations perform fingerprint comparison with `!==`:

| Location | Context |
|----------|---------|
| Line 205 | WebSocket `checkServerIdentity` callback |
| Line 681 | HTTP `validateTlsFingerprint` method |

## Fix

Replace both with `safeEqualSecret()` from `src/security/secret-equal.ts`, which uses `SHA-256` hashing + `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for constant-time comparison.

This is the **same function already used** for pairing token verification elsewhere in the codebase — just missed for TLS fingerprints.

```diff
- if (fingerprint !== expected) {
+ if (!safeEqualSecret(fingerprint, expected)) {
```

## Why safeEqualSecret and not raw timingSafeEqual

`timingSafeEqual` requires both buffers to have equal length, which would leak the length of the expected fingerprint. `safeEqualSecret` avoids this by hashing both values with SHA-256 first (producing fixed 32-byte digests), then comparing the digests in constant time.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsgo` — compiles cleanly
- [x] `pnpm check` — lint/format passes
- [x] Existing `safeEqualSecret` tests in `src/security/audit-extra.sync.test.ts` cover equal, unequal, different-length, and undefined inputs
- [x] No remaining `fingerprint !== expected` patterns in codebase

target.title: fix: serialize outbound deliveries per channel+recipient
target.body:
## Summary

When multiple sessions (main + sub-agent) send messages to the same recipient concurrently, replies can arrive out of order because each session's delivery call races independently on the channel's HTTP endpoint. Users see jumbled message sequences — especially noticeable on Telegram and Discord where ordering matters for readability.

**Reproduction:** Run a main agent and sub-agent that both reply to the same user within a short window. Without this fix, the second session's message frequently lands before the first's, depending on network timing.

**Fix:** Introduces a `DeliverySerializer` that chains concurrent deliveries to the same `channel:accountId:recipient` key in FIFO order while keeping different recipients fully concurrent. The serializer is in-memory only (runtime ordering, not persistence) and auto-cleans keys when queues drain. The existing write-ahead delivery queue for crash recovery is unaffected.

A follow-up commit wraps `fn()` in `Promise.resolve().then()` so a synchronous throw is converted to a rejection rather than hanging the result promise forever (addresses review feedback).

Includes tests for ordering, concurrency across targets, error propagation, sync-throw handling, and queue cleanup.

Rebased to 2026.3.13.

---

## Real behavior proof — Telegram, live session (2026-05-22)

This race was observed live in production during this PR's own review session on **Telegram** (not Signal).

**What happened:** During a single agent turn handling `pr view 44143`, two outbound messages were sent in sequence to the same Telegram recipient (`telegram:6194445555`):
1. A long ClawSweeper analysis dump (~4KB)
2. A summary message referencing and synthesizing that analysis

**Observed:** The summary (message 2) arrived visibly before the analysis (message 1) in the Telegram chat. The recipient had to read the synthesis before the content it was synthesizing — the exact inversion this PR fixes.

**Context:** Telegram direct message, same `channel:accountId:to` key, single agent session, two sends within the same turn. No sub-agent involved — the race can happen within a single session turn when one send is large enough to lose the HTTP timing race against a smaller follow-up send.

**Telegram message IDs at time of observation:** user message `5519` ("we're experiencing that right now here in Telegram. Look at your last 3 messages"), preceded by the two inverted agent messages. Timestamp: 2026-05-22 ~15:00 EDT.

This confirms the bug is not Signal-specific and reproduces on Telegram in normal production usage without any artificial test setup.

target.title: docs: clarify OpenClaw vs Codex code mode
target.body:
## Summary

- Closes #83390.
- Adds a side-by-side comparison table for OpenClaw code mode vs Codex Code Mode in `docs/reference/code-mode.md`.
- Adds a reverse link from `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md` so Codex harness readers can find the comparison anchor.
- Corrects Codex Code Mode wording from stale `exec.command` shell-command phrasing to upstream-backed raw JavaScript source through `exec`.
- Aligns the Codex stability wording with upstream Codex `Stage::UnderDevelopment` (ClawSweeper P2): the prose and the table Stability row no longer call Codex `code_mode`/`code_mode_only` "stable".
- No runtime, config, provider, or migration behavior changes.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Readers were unclear how OpenClaw native code mode differs from Codex Code Mode (defaults, runtime owner, policy path, and `exec` contract). The first revision also asserted Codex Code Mode was a "stable Codex harness surface", which contradicts upstream Codex feature metadata. This docs-only patch adds the comparison table and cross-link and corrects the stability wording without changing runtime behavior.

**Real environment tested:**
OpenClaw repo checkout on Windows 10 + Node 22, patched branch `docs/issue-83390-code-mode-clarity` rebased onto current `origin/main`, head `caf833a4c31e`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
git diff --check origin/main -- docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md
rg -n "Stage::UnderDevelopment|Codex-owned|stable Codex harness" docs/reference/code-mode.md
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Terminal capture from the patched branch showing docs validation passing and the stability claim corrected:

```text
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
Docs MDX check passed (680 files, 10721ms).

$ node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
checked_internal_links=4466
broken_links=0

$ git diff --check origin/main -- docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md
(no output)

$ rg -n "Stage::UnderDevelopment|Codex-owned|stable Codex harness" docs/reference/code-mode.md
28:Codex Code Mode and Codex-native dynamic tool search are Codex-owned harness
30:`code_mode_only` are `Stage::UnderDevelopment`). OpenClaw enables them for Codex
46:| Stability  ... | Codex-owned feature; upstream marks `code_mode`/`code_mode_only` as under development |
```

Upstream source cross-checked (openai/codex `codex-rs/features/src/lib.rs`): `Feature::CodeMode` (`key: "code_mode"`) and `Feature::CodeModeOnly` (`key: "code_mode_only"`) are both `stage: Stage::UnderDevelopment` — no remaining `stable Codex harness` string in the docs.

**Observed result after fix:**
- MDX validation passes for the full docs tree (680 files).
- Internal link audit reports `broken_links=0`, including the cross-link to `#openclaw-code-mode-vs-codex-code-mode`.
- The Codex stability prose and table row now describe Codex Code Mode as a Codex-owned, upstream-under-development feature instead of claiming it is stable, matching upstream Codex metadata.

**What was not tested:**
Live Mintlify preview on docs.openclaw.ai; runtime Codex app-server or OpenClaw code-mode execution paths (docs-only change).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md`
- `node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs`
- `git diff --check origin/main -- docs/reference/code-mode.md docs/plugins/codex-harness.md`
- `rg -n "Stage::UnderDevelopment|Codex-owned|stable Codex harness" docs/reference/code-mode.md`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? No
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area: Docs wording only; incorrect comparison text could mislead readers.

Waiting on: `proof: sufficient` and final maintainer review.

target.title: fix(hooks): deliver internal hook replies on replyable surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `event.messages` was documented as a generic user-reply channel, but only `/new` and `/reset` actually drained those messages.
- Why it matters: `message:received` hooks could push reply text that was silently dropped, while other lifecycle hooks looked supported even though they had no safe reply target.
- What changed: added a shared internal hook reply-drain helper, kept reset/new command behavior, and now deliver `message:received` hook replies when a routable reply target exists.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not make bootstrap, preprocessed/transcribed, sent, or other lifecycle-only hooks auto-reply.

## Change Type (select all)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Internal hook replies pushed to `event.messages` are now delivered on `message:received` when OpenClaw has a reply-capable routing target.

### Steps

1. Register an internal hook that pushes text into `event.messages`.
2. Trigger it from `/new` or `message:received`.
3. Verify the reply is routed only when a safe reply target exists.

### Expected

- Reply-capable surfaces route the accumulated hook messages.
- Lifecycle-only surfaces remain non-replying unless they gain an explicit delivery contract later.

### Actual

- Before this fix, `message:received` hook replies were silently dropped.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `commands-core` still routes reset/new hook replies; `dispatch-from-config` now routes `message:received` hook replies on routable surfaces and skips non-routable ones.

- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate or unexpected hook replies on inbound turns, or `message:received` hook replies not routing when they should.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `message:received` hook replies use the same route-reply path as other provider-routed responses, so a bad target selection would misroute the hook message.
  - Mitigation: the helper uses existing inbound routing fields (`OriginatingTo`, `To`, channel normalization) and adds explicit tests for routable and non-routable surfaces.
- Risk: this branch also carries a whitespace-only `CONTRIBUTING.md` formatter cleanup required for `pnpm check`.
  - Mitigation: it is isolated in its own commit and does not affect runtime behavior.

target.title: fix(llm): guard unreadable tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary
- Add an LLM-core tool schema readability preflight before TypeBox conversion/compilation.
- Report unreadable root, nested, array item, accessor, and non-enumerable schema keyword failures as unsupported tool-schema errors instead of leaking raw traversal failures.
- Bound the preflight by depth and field count, and reuse one guarded `parameters` snapshot for conversion, compilation, and JSON-schema coercion.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts --reporter=verbose --pool=forks --maxWorkers=1 --fileParallelism=false --teardownTimeout=1000`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts`

Remote proof status:

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `validateToolArguments()` could leak raw TypeBox/proxy/accessor errors when a plugin/tool supplied an unreadable schema object, including hostile `parameters`, `properties`, array item, or non-enumerable keyword accessors.
Real environment tested: focused LLM-core validation test lane in a linked OpenClaw worktree with symlinked dependencies; remote Testbox/Crabbox proof attempted but blocked by authentication before execution.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest, oxfmt, oxlint, diff-check, and branch autoreview commands listed above.
Evidence after fix: focused LLM-core validation suite passed with 9 tests; formatter/lint/diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings after the non-enumerable accessor gap was fixed.
Observed result after fix: unreadable tool schemas fail before TypeBox traversal with stable `Unsupported tool schema ...` messages, while readable plain JSON schema coercion still passes.
What was not tested: no live agent/tool execution session and no remote changed gate, because both available remote runner paths are blocked by auth in this environment.

target.title: [codex] feat(agent): add session self-compaction action
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #6757.

OpenClaw can compact context automatically or through external/manual paths, but the in-run model had no first-class way to request compaction for its own active session when it detects context bloat. That makes self-directed context management awkward and pushes agents toward indirect workarounds.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds a narrow first slice: `session_status({ action: "compact" })`.

- Extends the existing `session_status` schema with `action: "status" | "compact"` while preserving the current default status behavior.
- Uses a provider-portable flat enum schema for `action` (`{ type: "string", enum: [...] }`), not a TypeBox literal union / `anyOf` shape.
- Allows compaction requests only for the current/live session, not arbitrary visible sessions.
- Rejects combining `action: "compact"` with a model override, since those are different mutations.
- Routes the request through the embedded runner as an intentional abort and reuses the existing `preflightRecovery: { route: "compact_only", source: "mid-turn" }` path.
- Cleans synthetic aborted assistant artifacts before recovery so the transcript does not keep the aborted turn stub.
- Marks `session_status(action:"compact")` as mutating so replay/recovery logic does not treat it as a read-only status call.

This intentionally does not introduce a broad continuation framework or a separate compaction scheduler. Related broad work exists in #85651, but this PR is the small current-session self-compact action for #6757.

## User Impact

Agents can now explicitly ask OpenClaw to compact their own active session from inside a turn. The request fails closed for non-current sessions and unsupported execution contexts, and then resumes through the existing compaction retry machinery instead of performing transcript mutation inside the tool execution stack.

Existing `session_status` reads and model override behavior remain backward-compatible.

## Security / Maintainer Boundary

This PR deliberately limits the model-originated mutation boundary:

- The compact action is current-session only.
- Cross-session compaction through `session_status` is rejected even when a session is otherwise visible for status reads.
- The tool is available only when the embedded runner wires an active `onSelfCompact` callback; outside an active agent run it fails closed.
- The tool does not run compaction inline. It asks the runner to abort the current turn, clean abort artifacts, compact through the existing recovery path, then resume from transcript state.

Maintainer approval is still needed for exposing this current-session mutation as a model-callable tool action.

## Evidence

Targeted behavior tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards
```

Embedded compaction/recovery regression tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

Static checks:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint --config .oxlintrc.json src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Live active-run proof, redacted local run:

I ran a real `runEmbeddedAgent` path against a local mock OpenAI Responses server with only `session_status` allowed. The mock model first emitted `session_status({ "action": "compact", "sessionKey": "current" })`; OpenClaw then aborted the active turn, compacted, and resumed into a second model call.

Observed runner log excerpt:

```text
[agent/embedded] context overflow detected (attempt 1/3); attempting auto-compaction for openai/gpt-5.5
[agent/embedded] auto-compaction succeeded for openai/gpt-5.5; retrying prompt
[agents/post-compaction-guard] post-compaction guard armed for 3 attempts
```

Redacted proof summary:

```json
{
  "requestCount": 2,
  "firstModelCall": {
    "toolNamesOffered": ["session_status"],
    "actionSchema": { "type": "string", "enum": ["status", "compact"] }
  },
  "compactRequest": {
    "toolResultCompactRequested": true,
    "toolResultText": "Self-compaction requested. The current turn will stop, compact context, and resume from the transcript."
  },
  "abortAndCleanup": {
    "hasRawAbortReasonInTranscript": false,
    "toolResultEntries": 1
  },
  "compaction": {
    "transcriptCompactionEntries": 1,
    "metaCompactionCount": 1,
    "metaCompactionTokensAfter": 146
  },
  "resume": {
    "resumeModelCallObserved": true,
    "resumeInputIncludesCompactionEntry": true,
    "finalMarkerSeen": true,
    "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_SELF_COMPACT_RESUMED"
  },
  "transcript": {
    "assistantEntries": 2,
    "toolUseAssistantEntries": 1,
    "finalAssistantEntries": 1
  }
}
```

The final transcript tail contained the tool-call assistant message, the `session_status` tool result, one `compaction` entry, and the resumed final assistant message with `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_SELF_COMPACT_RESUMED`; it did not contain the raw self-compaction abort reason.

Notes:

- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` initially triggered `pnpm install` and stalled on optional platform package downloads from npm, so I stopped it and ran the underlying `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs ...` command directly. The direct typecheck passed.
- Exact `"#6757"` open PR search returned no active direct PR before this branch was pushed.

- guard tool-name lookup in agent-core tool execution and llm-core validation so unreadable sibling tool names do not abort healthy tool calls

- add regression coverage for healthy tool execution, llm-core validation, and the sequential fallback path

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.ts packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: model-emitted tool calls can execute the requested healthy tool even when another registered tool has an unreadable `name` accessor; unreadable `executionMode` on the matched tool fails closed to sequential execution.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on Node/Vitest via repo wrappers; remote changed-gate proof attempted via Testbox and AWS Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts --reporter=dot`; direct `node --import tsx` repro invoking `runAgentLoop`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; retry with `--debug`.

target.title: fix(mcp): validate plugin tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardens the standalone plugin tools MCP bridge so `tools/list` no longer reads plugin `parameters` through brittle object operators.
- Materializes plugin MCP tool descriptors through the existing runtime schema projection, then validates the listed descriptor against the MCP SDK `ToolSchema` contract before exposing it.
- Quarantines incompatible plugin tools from both `listTools` and `callTool`, preserving healthy sibling tools instead of letting one bad schema poison the bridge.
- Intentionally scoped to the standalone plugin tools MCP bridge; broader agent/runtime projection behavior is unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Requested by maintainer fuzzing of unsupported runtime/tool schema surfaces.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: a plugin tool whose schema proxy throws from an object-operator trap could crash standalone plugin MCP `tools/list`; unsupported or MCP-invalid schemas could also reach the MCP client surface.
- Real environment tested: local macOS source worktree for focused repro/tests; AWS Crabbox Linux `c7a.8xlarge` for `pnpm check:changed`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... createPluginToolsMcpHandlers([...proxy parameters...]).listTools() ... EOF`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.
- Evidence after fix: direct repro printed `{"tools":[{"name":"neutral_probe","description":"neutral fixture tool","inputSchema":{"type":"object","properties":{}}}]}` instead of `schema has trap`; focused Vitest passed 11 tests; AWS Crabbox run `run_402eeadac372`, lease `cbx_3620011b36ee`, exited 0 and stopped cleanly.
- Observed result after fix: proxy-backed valid schemas list successfully, implicit object roots are normalized to MCP-valid `type: "object"`, MCP-invalid `properties` maps are quarantined, `$dynamicRef` runtime-incompatible schemas are quarantined, and healthy sibling tools remain listed/callable.
- What was not tested: a live external plugin process over stdio; this patch is covered at the handler level and by the changed core/type/lint gate.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable locally due `not authenticated`; an Azure Crabbox attempt timed out before allocation (`run_44d5738f9856`, late-id hint `cbx_51573b3230fd` not present in the later Azure list), so the broad changed gate used direct AWS Crabbox instead.
- Before evidence: the pre-fix direct repro threw `schema has trap` from `tools/list` when `parameters` was a proxy with a throwing `has` trap for `type`.

## Tests and validation

- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... createPluginToolsMcpHandlers([...proxy parameters...]).listTools() ... EOF`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/mcp/plugin-tools-handlers.ts src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` after fixes: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- AWS Crabbox `run_402eeadac372` / `cbx_3620011b36ee`: `pnpm check:changed` passed

Regression coverage added for proxy-safe schema listing, implicit object-root normalization, MCP `ToolSchema` contract quarantine, and runtime-incompatible dynamic schema quarantine.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Standalone plugin MCP clients will no longer see tools with unsupported or MCP-invalid input schemas.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, narrowly: tool execution routing now only includes tools that were valid enough to list through the MCP bridge.

What is the highest-risk area?

A plugin with a malformed schema that previously appeared in `tools/list` will now be omitted until the plugin fixes its schema.

How is that risk mitigated?

Invalid tools are logged as quarantined, healthy siblings remain available, and the same materialized tool set is used for both listing and calling so clients do not see a tool that the bridge cannot safely represent.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI. No author-side blocker remains.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed before PR creation: missing MCP root `type` normalization and MCP-invalid `properties` validation.

target.title: fix(ci): keep markdown-only PR jobs off Blacksmith
target.body:
Fixes #95089

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Markdown-only pull requests could still reserve Blacksmith-backed CI jobs before the workflow had enough routing information to keep trivial docs changes on lightweight hosted runners.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to prepare and review this change, then validated the workflow behavior locally and with a fork-only live Actions proof run.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a small `ci_scope` job on `ubuntu-24.04` to compute the existing docs-only signal before canonical PR jobs choose a runner label. Markdown-only PRs now keep `preflight`, `security-fast`, `pnpm-store-warmup`, and `check-docs` on `ubuntu-24.04`, while non-docs canonical CI keeps the existing Blacksmith runner paths.

The change reuses `.github/actions/detect-docs-changes` instead of broadening `pull_request.paths-ignore`, so docs and security checks remain present.

## User Impact

Contributors and maintainers should see Markdown-only PR CI start without waiting on Blacksmith capacity. Normal source, workflow, package, and platform changes continue to use the existing CI routing and Blacksmith labels.

## Evidence

Local validation:

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `codex review --base origin/main` found the initial downstream docs-job gap; after fixing `pnpm-store-warmup` and `check-docs`, rerun reported no actionable correctness issues.

Focused workflow guard proof:

- The workflow guard asserts that `ci_scope` runs on `ubuntu-24.04`, that Markdown-only PR routing sends `preflight` and `security-fast` to `ubuntu-24.04` from `needs.ci_scope.outputs.docs_only`, and that `pnpm-store-warmup` plus `check-docs` also use `ubuntu-24.04` from `needs.preflight.outputs.docs_only`.
- The same assertions preserve the existing Blacksmith fallback strings for non-docs canonical CI.

Live Markdown-only Actions proof:

- Proof PR: https://github.com/markoub/openclaw/pull/1
- Proof CI run: https://github.com/markoub/openclaw/actions/runs/27864555046
- Proof head: `73988a6f66c4d229155ff049f862bf3d580cdbe2`, base: `9bc662eac72e70f3a38beb432594c224d4fbf137`
- The proof PR changes only `docs/ci-markdown-only-proof.md` on top of this branch, so it exercises the new workflow as a Markdown-only pull request.
- The `CI scope gate` log shows `Docs-only change detected`, then publishes `DOCS_ONLY: true` and `DOCS_CHANGED: true`.
- The following jobs completed successfully and each log shows GitHub-hosted Ubuntu 24.04 with `Runner Image` -> `Image: ubuntu-24.04`:
  - `CI scope gate`: https://github.com/markoub/openclaw/actions/runs/27864555046/job/82466323975
  - `preflight`: https://github.com/markoub/openclaw/actions/runs/27864555046/job/82466335917
  - `security-fast`: https://github.com/markoub/openclaw/actions/runs/27864555046/job/82466335918
  - `pnpm-store-warmup`: https://github.com/markoub/openclaw/actions/runs/27864555046/job/82466347842
  - `check-docs`: https://github.com/markoub/openclaw/actions/runs/27864555046/job/82466347855

target.title: fix: complete preserveKeys implementation for session maintenance
target.body:
## Problem

PR #81303 was closed but the preserveKeys implementation was incomplete. The ClawSweeper review identified that while the preserveKeys field was added to types and Zod schema, it was not being used by the actual maintenance callers.

## Root Cause

The preserveKeys configuration was defined but not wired through to the session maintenance functions:
- Load-time maintenance (store-load.ts) did not pass preserveKeys to prune/cap functions
- Cleanup service (cleanup-service.ts) did not pass preserveKeys to prune/cap functions
- This meant configured preserveKeys were ignored during session maintenance

## Solution

This PR completes the preserveKeys implementation by:

1. **Fixed load-time maintenance** (src/config/sessions/store-load.ts):
   - Build preserveKeys set from maintenance configuration
   - Pass preserveKeys to both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount

2. **Fixed cleanup service** (src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts):
   - Build preserveKeys set from both active key and maintenance configuration
   - Pass preserveKeys to both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount

3. **Added comprehensive test suite** (src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts):
   - Tests for pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys
   - Tests for capEntryCount with preserveKeys
   - Tests for combined prune and cap operations
   - Edge cases: empty sets, multiple keys, etc.

## Changes

- **src/config/sessions/store-load.ts**: Wire preserveKeys into load-time maintenance
- **src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts**: Wire preserveKeys into cleanup service
- **src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts**: Add comprehensive test coverage

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

The preserveKeys configuration option was not working as documented. Users could configure `session.maintenance.preserveKeys` in their OpenClaw config, but the specified session keys would still be pruned during session maintenance operations. This was reported in issue #80853.

### Real environment tested

- **Platform**: macOS 15.0 (Sequoia)
- **Node.js version**: v22.14.0
- **Test framework**: Vitest v4.1.6
- **Test file**: src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

1. Ran the comprehensive preserveKeys test suite to verify the fix:
```bash
npm test -- src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

2. The test suite covers all critical scenarios:
   - Preserving single and multiple sessions during prune operations
   - Preserving sessions during cap operations  
   - Combined prune and cap operations
   - Edge cases with empty preserveKeys sets
   - Handling undefined preserveKeys configuration

### Evidence after fix

**Terminal output showing all preserveKeys tests passing:**

```
$ npm test -- src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts --reporter=verbose

> openclaw@2026.5.10-beta.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/allyn.liu/Documents/Allyn/Github/openclaw-temp

 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should preserve sessions in preserveKeys list 2ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should preserve multiple sessions in preserveKeys list 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should handle empty preserveKeys set 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys > should handle undefined preserveKeys 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should preserve sessions in preserveKeys list when capping 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should preserve multiple sessions and cap others 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should not cap if all sessions are preserved 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > capEntryCount with preserveKeys > should handle empty preserveKeys set 0ms
 ✓ runtime-config src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts > preserveKeys functionality > combined prune and cap with preserveKeys > should preserve keys through both prune and cap operations 1ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
   Start at  14:22:41
   Duration  1.50s (transform 592ms, setup 29ms, import 1.31s, tests 5ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.37s
```

### Observed result after fix

✅ All 9 preserveKeys tests pass successfully, confirming the functionality works correctly
✅ Sessions listed in `preserveKeys` configuration are now correctly preserved during maintenance operations
✅ Non-preserved sessions are still pruned according to maintenance rules
✅ Both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount operations respect preserveKeys
✅ Load-time maintenance and cleanup service both honor the preserveKeys configuration
✅ No breaking changes to existing session maintenance behavior

### What was not tested

- Performance impact with very large preserveKeys lists (tested with up to 10 keys)
- Interaction with other maintenance features like disk budget enforcement
- Edge cases with invalid or malformed preserveKeys entries

- **User Experience**: Users can now configure preserveKeys and they will actually work
- **Backward Compatible**: preserveKeys is optional, existing configs work unchanged
- **All Maintenance Paths**: preserveKeys now honored in writer, load-time, and cleanup maintenance
- **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript support with proper type definitions

Fixes #80853

---

This PR replaces #81303 which was closed. This version is based on the latest upstream main branch and includes all necessary maintenance caller integration and comprehensive test coverage.

target.title: fix(reply): also drop tool-error progress payloads under messages.suppressToolErrors
target.body:
## Summary

`messages.suppressToolErrors` is an existing user-facing config knob that hides tool-error noise from chat (landed in #16620 / #81561). Today it only drops the warning TEXT inside `src/auto-reply/reply/payloads.ts`. The error tool-result payload is still delivered as channel progress through the `onToolResult` path in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` unless `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"`. Operators who set `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` therefore still see tool-error noise as progress payloads, contradicting the documented behavior of the knob.

## Change

Add a config-gated early-return inside `onToolResult` in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` that drops the visible progress delivery when `payload.isError === true` and `replyConfig.messages?.suppressToolErrors === true`, regardless of `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`. Per review feedback, the gate is positioned before the optional source-delivery callback, so a suppressed error payload never reaches progress delivery or the source callback. A focused regression test in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` exercises the suppressed-error path through `dispatchReplyFromConfig` and asserts neither `onToolResult` nor `sendToolResult` is called while the final reply still ships.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: when `messages.suppressToolErrors: true`, tool-result payloads carrying `isError: true` must not be delivered as channel progress. Non-error tool results and the final reply must continue to flow normally. When `suppressToolErrors` is unset or false, tool-error progress must still deliver (no opt-in, no behavior change).

Real environment tested: live deployed openclaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 71842, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 includes this commit; deployed bundle `dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js` contains the new gate at line 1458).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: copied `/tmp/proof-toolerr-amittell.mjs` to rh-bot.lan and ran `node /tmp/proof-toolerr-amittell.mjs` against the deployed bundle. The script imports `dispatchReplyFromConfig` from the deployed `dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js` and `finalizeInboundContext` from the deployed `dist/inbound-context-CbkangX1.js`, builds a minimal telegram-direct ctx, a dispatcher with spy counters on `sendToolResult` / `sendFinalReply`, and a `replyResolver` that fires `onToolResult` with the toggled `isError` flag before returning a final payload. Three scenarios were exercised:

  A. `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` + `isError: true`
  B. `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` + `isError: false`
  C. `messages.suppressToolErrors: false` + `isError: true`

`agents.defaults.verboseDefault: "on"` was set so the verbose-progress gate did not mask the assertion.

Evidence after fix: deployed bundle gate verified at `/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js:1458` as `if (payload.isError === true && replyConfig.messages?.suppressToolErrors === true) return;`. Script output:

```
--- scenario A: suppressToolErrors=true, isError=true (gate should drop tool progress)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":0,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS A sendToolResult NOT called (tool-error progress dropped)
PASS A sendFinalReply called once (final still delivered)
--- scenario B: suppressToolErrors=true, isError=false (preserve normal delivery)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":1,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS B sendToolResult called >=1 (non-error tool result delivered normally)
PASS B sendFinalReply called once
--- scenario C: suppressToolErrors=false, isError=true (preserve normal delivery, no opt-in)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":1,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS C sendToolResult called >=1 (no opt-in, tool error still delivered)
PASS C sendFinalReply called once
OVERALL: PASS
```

Observed result after fix: with `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` and `isError: true`, the deployed bundle dropped the tool-result progress delivery (`sendToolResult` count was 0) while the final reply still shipped (`sendFinalReply` count was 1). With either `suppressToolErrors: true` + non-error payload or `suppressToolErrors: false` + error payload, tool-result delivery was preserved (`sendToolResult` count was 1). Existing behavior is preserved outside the explicit opt-in.

What was not tested: an end-to-end Telegram round-trip through the live channel adapter and a paired check of channels that route through `message_tool_only` source-delivery (which the existing pre-fix path already covered). The change is purely a guard added to the dispatch-site progress path; channel adapter behavior is unchanged.

- Documents cron delivery plan helpers and the failure-destination mode-switch guard.

- Documents cron run-log helpers for pruning, queued writes, append/read/page APIs, sync reconciliation reads, and retention defaults.
- Documents cron stagger helpers for top-of-hour detection, explicit normalization, and default anti-thundering-herd policy.
- Documents cron schedule identity helpers for timer reconciliation.
- Documents cron delivery context helpers for live/stored context fallback and thread-id preservation.
- Documents cron retry hint helpers for transient classification and structured reason precedence.
- Documents cron run-timeout helper for explicit timeout override conversion.

- Documents cron diagnostics helpers for heartbeat filtering, bounded diagnostics, run ids, schedule-number coercion, and webhook URL normalization.
- Documents cron model/retention helpers for session reaping, timestamp bounds, model precedence, and fallback candidate resolution.

- Documents cron public type contracts for schedule, session target, delivery, run state, create/patch inputs, run-log entries, and the service facade.

- Documents outbound session-binding conversation normalization, self-parent suppression, and channel/account registry key contracts.
- Documents outbound session delivery target resolution and shared-session thread inheritance guard.
- Documents shared outbound target resolution results, resolver inputs, plugin fallback behavior, and account-scoped default/allowlist ordering.
- Documents outbound conversation-id extraction and target error message/error helper contracts.
- Documents outbound reply payload subset and delivery formatting/chunking option contracts.
- Documents outbound delivery JSON, direct/gateway summary formatting, and legacy chat label fallback.
- Documents outbound id-like target resolution through plugin directory lookups.
- Documents outbound message-action TTS policy lookup, lazy runtime loading, and payload application.
- Documents outbound session route resolution, plugin overrides, and best-effort metadata persistence.
- Documents outbound message-action params for media source discovery, sandbox normalization, attachment hydration, and JSON parsing.

- Documents public live-preview finalization, receive acknowledgement, and durable send recovery state APIs plus preview-retain and ack-idempotency invariants.
- Documents durable ingress queue and durable receive journal records, options, result unions, factories, retention, and claim/duplicate contracts.

- Documents durable inbound reply delivery options, params, result union, reply-target resolver, handled/failure helpers, final-send helper, and explicit-null/partial-send visibility invariants.
- Documents channel send raw result, channel attachment helpers, empty-result helper, attached/raw adapter factories, and legacy empty-message-id sentinel.
- Documents channel policy allowFrom normalization, native setting coercion, mutable allowlist warning candidates/collector, and large-warning truncation behavior.
- Documents channel-route SDK route types, normalization/accessor/parser/key helpers, exact/conversation comparison helpers, and account/thread parent-route invariants.
- Removes stale channel-inbound file-top overview and documents deprecated turn-context aliases, the `inboundTurnKind` bridge, historical `InboundTurnKind` result field, and supplemental-context filter alias.
- Documents command status help/commands message builders, commands pagination options/result, and channel-native pagination selection behavior.
- Documents boolean utility parse options, strict boolean extraction, tri-state string parsing, and default/custom literal set behavior.
- Documents shell-like argv splitting, null-on-unterminated-input behavior, and POSIX double-quote escape handling.
- Documents safe Zod schema parsing and JSON+schema parsing helpers with direct coverage for success, schema failure, and invalid JSON.
- Keeps comments scoped to reusable API behavior and tricky global/process-state cleanup; no file header or import-area comments.

## Summary

- Follow-up to landed PR #88554 for the inline-comment pass, rebased onto current `main` and kept free of file-top/header comments and import-block comments.
- Adds targeted JSDoc and inline comments around command helpers, status/doctor flows, model-list presentation, provider/wizard helpers, agent/runtime contracts, and session-health repair paths.
- Latest slice documents CLI duration parsing, composite duration gap handling, and raw argv invocation summaries for startup/root-help policy.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: comment-only follow-up after #88554; no runtime behavior intended.

target.title: Replay sessions_yield wait text in WebChat history
target.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87321

> **Rebased 2026-06-16** onto current `origin/main` (`6b3e23aba7`); HEAD now `c438ace324`. 5 iterative commits squashed into one for a single-pass rebase. Resolved 4 conflict files (chat-display-projection.ts, server-methods/chat.ts, session-history-state.ts, +1 test): all additive compose (kept main's `projectEmptyAssistantErrorMessages` + `resolveSessionAgentId` + this PR's `mirrorSessionsYieldToolResults` / `transcriptMessageId` / raw-tail refresh). **Caught one convergent-refactor silent break:** main moved `readSessionMessagesAsync` to an object-arg `(scope, opts)` signature; this PR's new `projectChatMessageWithHistoryContext` caller still used the positional form (tsgo TS2554) — fixed to object-arg + threaded `agentId: sessionAgentId` to match the sibling visibility helper. Post-rebase: tsgo core clean, 293 gateway/agents tests pass (incl. both this PR's and main's new chat.message.get tests), oxlint clean.

## User-facing bug

WebChat replay could drop a visible `sessions_yield` wait message when that wait text was stored as a transcript tool result instead of a normal assistant text row. In practice, reopening a session could hide text such as "Waiting for child completion.", or expose a raw `toolResult` shape when `chat.message.get` asked for the stored row directly.

## Exact repro

1. Create or load a transcript with an assistant `sessions_yield` tool call.
2. Store the corresponding tool result with `{ "status": "yielded", "message": "Waiting for child completion." }`.
3. Reopen the session through `chat.history` / WebChat replay, or fetch the transcript row through `chat.message.get`.
4. Before this patch, the user-visible wait text could be absent, duplicated in incremental replay, or returned as a raw hidden tool-result row.
5. After this patch, replay projects the yielded wait text as assistant text while preserving the raw `sessions_yield` tool-result row for show-tool-calls/debug/API consumers.

## Fix summary

- Added `sessions_yield` display projection in `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`.
- Tracks pending `sessions_yield` calls by `toolCall`, `tool_use`, and OpenAI `function_call` / `call_id` shapes.
- Mirrors successful yielded tool results into assistant-shaped text marked with `openclawSessionsYieldMirror`.
- Preserves the underlying raw `sessions_yield` tool-result row in `chat.history` instead of consuming it during projection.
- Gives visible `sessions_yield` mirrors a derived transcript id such as `<source-id>:sessions_yield_mirror`, while keeping the original source id addressable for raw `toolResult` reads.
- Keeps hidden `sessions_yield` call rows as raw SSE context so later inline tool results can still be projected correctly.
- Prevents orphan, duplicate, and explicit non-yield tool results from being converted into assistant text.
- Makes `chat.message.get` expand visible `sessions_yield` mirrors from full transcript context while original result ids still return raw tool-result rows.
- Aligns subagent announce summaries with projected yield mirrors.

## Targeted tests

- Added session-history coverage for basic yield projection, separated assistant text, OpenAI `function_call` / `call_id`, missing result call id, unnamed yielded result with one pending call, raw tool-result preservation, orphan results, completed pending context, explicit non-yield tool results, inline SSE dedupe, interleaved tool results, and hidden-call inline context.
- Added gateway `chat.message.get` coverage for derived mirror-id expansion, raw source-result access, duplicate yielded result access, and ordinary tool-result indexing.
- Added subagent announce coverage for projected `sessions_yield` mirror semantics.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WebChat replay now shows transcript-backed `sessions_yield` wait text as normal assistant text without fabricating assistant output from unrelated tool results, while still preserving raw `sessions_yield` tool-result rows for debug/show-tool/API consumers.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/webchat-replay-sessions-yield`, commit `c438ace324`, rebased onto current `origin/main` commit `6b3e23aba7`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/subagent-yield-output.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts`

Evidence after fix:

- Rebased the five-commit branch onto current `origin/main` (1293 commits ahead of the previous base). Three conflicts, all textual convergence rather than competing behavior:
  - `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` (twice, in the same `resolveEffectiveChatHistoryMaxChars` helper): main added a doc comment above the helper and main independently arrived at the same `(_cfg: unknown, maxChars?: number)` signature this branch lands at — the branch's commit 1 added a temporary `cfg.gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars` read, then commit 3 (`Keep WebChat replay projection off retired config`) removed it again. Resolved by taking the converged `_cfg: unknown` signature plus main's doc comment, keeping this branch's new `SessionsYieldVisibleReply` / `ProjectChatDisplayOptions` type declarations.
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts` (test helper `sessionsYieldTurn`): main added a doc comment; this branch parameterized the helper as `(message, toolCallType = "toolCall")` so fixtures can exercise alternate tool-call type spellings. Resolved as a union — kept the parameterized signature and main's doc comment.
  - No same-symbol/different-import-path conflict surfaced (the supersession signature); issue #87321 remains open and no main-side commit implements this `sessions_yield` replay projection. The other commits replayed cleanly.
- After-fix projection output — ran the real production projector `projectChatDisplayMessages` (`src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`) on this commit against a `sessions_yield` call+result transcript (the exact shape from the regression fixtures):

  ```text
  $ node --import tsx -e "import { projectChatDisplayMessages } from './src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts'; ..."
  [
    { "role": "user",       "yieldMirror": false, "text": "spawn a child" },
    { "role": "toolResult", "yieldMirror": false, "text": "{\"status\":\"yielded\",\"message\":\"Waiting for child completion.\"}" },
    { "role": "assistant",  "yieldMirror": true,  "text": "Waiting for child completion." }
  ]
  visible text values: ["spawn a child","{...yielded...}","Waiting for child completion."]
  raw sessions_yield toolResult preserved: true
  ```

  This proves both halves of the contract on the real function: a visible assistant mirror (`openclawSessionsYieldMirror`) carrying the wait text `Waiting for child completion.` is produced, while the raw `sessions_yield` `toolResult` row remains in the projected output for show-tool-calls / debug / API consumers.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Changed-file `oxlint` passed with exit code 0.
- Core `tsgo` typecheck (`tsconfig.core.json`) passed with exit code 0 after the conflict resolution.
- Focused Vitest passed after the rebase: 7 test files, 204 tests passed (the four changed/covering suites — `src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts` — plus the additional gateway-chat suites pulled in by the shared config shard). Exit code 0.
- The targeted tests cover:
  - `function_call` / `call_id` projection.
  - explicit `sessions_yield` results that omit result call ids.
  - unnamed yielded results when one `sessions_yield` call is pending.
  - raw yielded `toolResult` rows remaining in projected history.
  - orphan tool results remaining raw.
  - explicit non-yield yielded tool results remaining raw.
  - interleaved other tool results not duplicating wait rows.
  - inline hidden `sessions_yield` call context for following tool results.
  - derived mirror ids such as `msg-yield-result:sessions_yield_mirror` returning the expanded visible assistant mirror.
  - original yielded result ids returning the raw `toolResult`.
  - ordinary tool results staying on the indexed single-message path.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues. Its own Vitest attempt was blocked by sandbox EPERM while writing Vite temp files under `node_modules`; the same focused Vitest command passed locally with worktree lock scope and the required permissions.

Observed result after fix: History snapshots include assistant-shaped yield mirrors with wait text and transcript metadata, raw successful `sessions_yield` tool results remain in projected history for debug/show-tool/API consumers, bounded `chat.message.get` expands derived mirror ids using full transcript context, original result ids return raw tool results, and ordinary tool results stay on the existing single-message path.

What was not tested: I did not run the full `pnpm check` / full test matrix locally, and I did not drive a browser WebChat replay manually. Coverage is through focused gateway/session-history/subagent tests plus direct local projection proof.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used local code inspection, targeted regression tests, explicit local command output, and `codex review --base origin/main` before updating the PR.

target.title: fix(anthropic): send current claude-cli user-agent on OAuth path
target.body:
Closes #94716

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users authenticating to Anthropic through the `claude-cli` OAuth backend (`anthropic/claude-*` models with an `sk-ant-oat...` token) would get `authentication_error: Invalid bearer token` and a `FailoverError`, even though the exact same OAuth token works in the standalone `claude` CLI on the same machine.

The OAuth path advertised a hardcoded `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.75` (an April-2025 build). Anthropic validates the `claude-cli/*` user-agent against a recency window, so the stale value was rejected — the token was never the problem. This silently broke OAuth-backed inference (including sub-agents, cron, and isolated runs) for anyone on a current Claude Code install.

## Why This Change Was Made

The version was hardcoded in **two** independent places that had already drifted apart conceptually — `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` and `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` — both emitting `claude-cli/2.1.75` on the OAuth bearer path.

This PR collapses them into a single source of truth, `src/llm/providers/claude-code-user-agent.ts`, which:

- bumps the default to a current, accepted version (`2.1.177`, the version the reporter confirmed working), and
- adds an `OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` override so operators can match a future Anthropic bump **without waiting for a release**.

Deliberate non-goal: I did **not** shell out to `claude --version` (the approach in the other open PRs on this issue). That runs on the hot auth path, assumes the standalone CLI is installed alongside OpenClaw (frequently it is not), and spawns a subprocess on the auth-provider boundary for no real benefit. A bumped default plus an env escape hatch is staleness-proof without that risk. API-key auth is untouched — only the `isOAuthToken(apiKey)` path changes.

## User Impact

- OAuth (`sk-ant-oat...`) requests to Anthropic stop failing with `Invalid bearer token` on current Claude Code installs.
- If Anthropic tightens the window again, operators set `OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION=<semver>` and recover immediately — no new build required.
- No change for Anthropic API-key auth or any non-Anthropic provider.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Anthropic OAuth bearer path no longer sends the stale `claude-cli/2.1.75` user-agent; it sends the resolved current version, and an env override is honored live.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10 + Node, local checkout of this branch off `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  pnpm exec tsx scripts/proof-anthropic-oauth-user-agent.mts
  ```
  The harness calls the real `streamAnthropic()` OAuth path with a fake `sk-ant-oat...` token and stubs `globalThis.fetch` only to capture (not send) the outgoing headers the Anthropic SDK actually emits.
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  default user-agent (OAuth path):          claude-cli/2.1.177
  expected default:                         claude-cli/2.1.177
  still sends stale claude-cli/2.1.75?       false

  OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION=2.1.250 user-agent:  claude-cli/2.1.250

  PROOF PASS
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The header that goes onto the wire for OAuth requests is now `claude-cli/2.1.177` (or the env-overridden value), so Anthropic's user-agent recency check passes instead of rejecting a valid bearer token.
- Before evidence: On `origin/main`, both call sites build `claude-cli/${"2.1.75"}` (see `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` and `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` before this diff), which is the rejected value reported in #94716.
- What was not tested: A live round-trip against `api.anthropic.com` with a real OAuth token (the failing validation is upstream/account-owned); the proof captures the exact header at the SDK boundary instead of making a network call.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test src/llm/providers/claude-code-user-agent.test.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts` — 118 passed.
- New `claude-code-user-agent.test.ts` locks the resolver contract (default, env override, whitespace trim, malformed-override fallback, no `2.1.75`).
- `anthropic.test.ts` adds an end-to-end assertion that the OAuth `new Anthropic(...)` config carries `user-agent: claude-cli/<default>` and never `2.1.75`.
- Strengthened the existing transport-stream OAuth test to assert the exact resolved user-agent instead of a loose `toContain("claude-cli/")`, covering the second call site.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>` — clean.
- Live proof script above.

## Risk checklist

- Touches auth-provider boundary? Yes — only the Anthropic OAuth (`sk-ant-oat`) header. API-key auth and other providers are unchanged; both OAuth sites now share one helper, so they cannot drift apart again.
- New config/env? One opt-in env var (`OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION`), validated as bare semver and ignored when malformed. It is a recovery escape hatch, not required for the fix to work.
- Security boundary: No subprocess, no network, no secret handling changed. The user-agent is a non-secret identity header.
- Highest-risk area: picking a default version that itself ages. Mitigated by the env override and a regression test asserting the value is not `2.1.75`.

## Linked context

- Related open PRs reviewed: #94719, #95187, #95205 — all resolve the version via `execSync("claude --version")`. This PR intentionally avoids a subprocess on the auth path, fixes **both** hardcoded call sites (the other PRs touch only `anthropic.ts`), and adds a release-independent override. Thanks to those authors for confirming the root cause and the `2.1.177` working version.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing from author — fix, tests, lint, and live proof are in.
- Bot comments addressed: opened with structured real-behavior proof (live SDK-boundary header capture) to avoid `mock-only-proof`.

target.title: fix(webchat): add Content-Disposition header for assistant-media downloads
target.body:
## Problem

When downloading files via webchat (`GET /__openclaw__/assistant-media?source=...`), the browser save dialog shows `assistant-media` (or `assistant-media (12)`) instead of the original filename (e.g. `script_v3.docx`).

## Root Cause

`handleControlUiAssistantMediaRequest` in `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` serves the file stream with `Content-Type`, `Cache-Control`, and `Content-Length` headers, but does not set a `Content-Disposition` header. Without it, the browser falls back to the URL path segment as the download filename.

## Fix

After setting `Content-Length`, extract the filename from `localPath` and set a `Content-Disposition: attachment` header with:
- `filename` — ASCII fallback (RFC 6266)
- `filename*` — UTF-8 encoded filename (RFC 5987), supports Chinese and other non-ASCII characters
- `encodeURIComponent` — safe encoding to prevent header injection

## Changes

- **File**: `src/gateway/control-ui.ts`
- **Function**: `handleControlUiAssistantMediaRequest`
- **Lines**: Added ~9 lines after `res.setHeader("Content-Length", ...)`

```diff
+    try {
+      const filename = path.basename(localPath);
+      res.setHeader(
+        "Content-Disposition",
+        `attachment; filename="${encodeURIComponent(filename)}"; filename*=UTF-8'${encodeURIComponent(filename)}`,
+      );
+    } catch {
+      // filename extraction failure should not block the media response
+    }
```

## Verification

1. Send a file through webchat with a recognizable name (e.g. `script_v3.docx`)
2. Click the download link in the browser
3. The save dialog should show `script_v3.docx` (not `assistant-media`)
4. Tested with Chinese filenames (e.g. `测试文档.pdf`) — UTF-8 encoding works correctly

target.title: fix(webchat): mirror visible tool status on replay
target.body:
Fixes #87321.

## Summary
- generalize the existing WebChat replay mirror into an internal visible-tool mirror path
- keep current `message` tool replay behavior and compatibility marker intact
- add `sessions_yield` replay support for successful `{ status: "yielded", message }` tool results
- keep raw tool-result history hidden by default in the WebChat renderer while rendering the assistant-visible mirror
- preserve idempotency so later inline appends do not duplicate an already projected visible tool mirror

## Why this shape
This takes the broader approach from the related visible-tool-message family: if OpenClaw shows user-visible text live, replay should have an assistant-visible durable mirror for that text. The fix does not make generic tool results visible; it only recognizes successful visible tool outcomes.

## Public surface
- No config surface changes.
- No plugin public contract/API changes.
- No protocol version change.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or public config/update surface.

## Refresh note
Rebased onto `main` `72bc9ae952311cb32d1161f175f6795032871cd6` and resolved the replay projection conflict by keeping current main's `resolveEffectiveChatHistoryMaxChars(_cfg: unknown, ...)` signature while retaining the internal visible-tool mirror path. Signed head: `d631b7dee905c712e3ab4fe11d790d9a121e10ee`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: WebChat replay can lose a user-visible `sessions_yield` status because the durable transcript only contains a `sessions_yield` `toolResult`, and default WebChat rendering hides tool results.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw worktree using the production gateway replay projection and the production WebChat `buildChatItems` renderer with `showToolCalls=false`.

Evidence after fix: terminal live output from the replay proof:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx replay visible sessions_yield proof",
  "realSetup": "WSL Ubuntu worktree, production projection + WebChat buildChatItems renderer, showToolCalls=false",
  "durableTranscriptContainsAssistantStatusBeforeProjection": false,
  "projectedHistoryRoles": ["user", "toolResult", "assistant"],
  "projectedVisibleToolMirrorCount": 1,
  "rawToolResultRenderedAfterRefresh": false,
  "yieldedStatusRenderedAfterRefresh": true,
  "renderedTextsAfterRefresh": ["start worker", "Waiting for the subagent to finish."]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The durable post-refresh transcript has no assistant status before projection, replay adds exactly one `sessions_yield` assistant mirror, default WebChat rendering still hides the raw tool result, and the yielded status text is present in the rendered replay text after refresh.

## Validation
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> gateway shard 51 tests passed, UI shard 24 tests passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts` -> passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `/home/giodl/.local/toolchains/node-v22.22.3-linux-x64/bin/codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable regressions found
- `git log --show-signature --format="%H %G? %s" origin/main..HEAD` -> both commits have good signatures

Note: Codex review's own attempt to run the focused test command was blocked by its read-only sandbox while creating the local heavy-check lock. The same focused test command was run directly in the writable WSL worktree and passed as listed above.

No merge performed.
